one_time_description: Land and building contributed by Kahkewistahaw 1907 Specific Claim Trust ($4,428,000); also allowance on loan receivable from Kihew Fab-Co Ltd. ($3,128,976)
economic_notes: First Nation operates Birds Point Resort Limited Partnership, Weyotisiwin Holdings LP, Kihew Fab-Co Ltd. (50%), gas station/convenience store/car wash under construction, hotel partnership. Flood claim and treaty benefit claim against Government of Canada ongoing. Kahkewistahaw Development LP has construction contracts for gas station, convenience store and car wash (total $3,199,054).
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2022. Province is Saskatchewan (Treaty 4 Territory). Kihew Fab-Co Ltd. changed from modified equity to proportionate consolidation (50%) during the year as it ceased to be self-sustaining - no retroactive restatement. Allowance of $3,128,976 recorded on Kihew Fab-Co loan receivable. Land and building contributed by Specific Claim Trust ($4,428,000) recorded as non-cash item. Ongoing flood claim and treaty benefit claim against Canada. Kahkewistahaw 1907 Specific Claim Trust - Trust Account excluded from consolidation as not controlled by Chief and Council. Two entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs): Kahkewistahaw Development LP and Kahkewistahaw Management LP. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($17,903,944) + CMHC ($1,454,409) + ISC-FNIHB ($282,621) = $19,640,974 plus Yorkton Tribal Council ($840,841) + YTC Child and Family Services ($55,753) + Enbridge grants ($0) = adjusted figure used. OSR calculated as total revenue minus identified government/institutional transfers.
